info.title: Disable Prevention Rule Public APIs
info.description:
The Disable Prevention Rule API lets you manage Disable Prevention rules in Cortex Cloud. These rules allow you to define specific exceptions to prevention actions enforced on endpoints.

You can apply Disable Prevention rules only to endpoints running Cortex XDR agent version 7.9 or later.

Using this API, you can:

- Retrieve existing Disable Prevention rules with support for filtering and pagination.
- Retrieve the list of available modules for a specified platform.
- Create new Disable Prevention rules to configure granular exceptions to endpoint prevention actions.
- Update existing Disable Prevention rules.
- Delete Disable Prevention rules by their IDs.

You can export the OpenAPI specs in the [Cortex Stoplight instance](https://cortex-panw.stoplight.io/docs/cortex-xsiam-3-x/adidmqwfho4b5-disable-prevention-rule-public-ap-is).

**Required license:** In Cortex XSIAM Premium, Cortex XSIAM Enterprise, or Cortex XSIAM NG SIEM, requires the Cortex Cloud Posture Management add-on.

post.description: Returns a list of Disable Prevention rules based on filters, sorting, and pagination.
Returns a list of available modules for a specific platform.
Creates a new Disable Prevention rule.
Updates an existing Disable Prevention rule.
Deletes one or more Disable Prevention rules based on the provided rule ID.

search_from.description: Zero-based index of the first Disable Prevention rule to return. Used for pagination.

search_to.description: Index number of Disable Prevention rules to return starting from `search_from`.

field.description: Rule attribute to apply the filter on. For example: `rule_id`, `status`, `modification_time`, `platform`).
Rule attribute to apply the filter on. For example: `rule_id`, `status`, `modification_time`, `platform`).

operator.description: Comparison operator used for filtering. For example, (`eq`, `neq`, `gte`, `lte`, `contains` or `not_contains`)

value.description:
The value to compare against the specified field. The expected data type and format depend on the field being filtered and the operator used.

**Examples:**

* If the `field` is `status`, the `value` can be `enabled` or `disabled`.
* If the `field` is `modification_time`, the `value` should be a Unix timestamp in milliseconds (for example, **`1762870187000`**).
* If the `field` is `platform`, the `value` can be a string such as `windows`, `macos`, or `linux`

filter_count.description: The number of rules returned in the current response after applying filters.

total_count.description: The total number of rules available that match the filter criteria, regardless of pagination.

reply.description: Returns the Disable Prevention Rule ID.
Describes the result of the API operation.

Example 1.description: Data prevention rules for my mac

rule_id.description: The unique identifier of the Disable Prevention rule.

rule_name.description: Name of the Disable Prevention rule.

description.description: Description explaining the purpose and behavior of the rule.
description.example: Data prevention rules for my mac

platform.description: Indicates the operating system to which the rule applies. For example, `windows`, `linux`, or `macos`

conditions.description: A combination of parameters configured during rule creation.

path.description: Path to the required files or folders

module_ids.description: A list of module ids associated with the rule.

profile_ids.description: A list of profile ids to which the rule is applied.

scope.description: Specifies the scope of the rule, such as `global` (All endpoints) or `profile` (Exception profiles).

status.description: Current state of the rule (for example, `enabled` or `disabled`).

created_by.description: Name of the user who created the rule.

user_email.description: Email address of the user associated with the creation or last modification of the rule.

modification_time.description: Timestamp when the rule was last modified.

platform.description: Indicates the operating system to which the rule applies. For example, `windows`, `linux`, or `macos`.

module_id.description: Module ID associated with the rule.

description.description: Description of the disable prevention rule.

conditions_definition.description: A combination of parameters configured during rule creation.

path.description: Path to the required files or folders

rule_id.description: The unique identifier of the Disable Prevention rule.

rule_name.description: Name of the disable prevention rule.

description.description: Description explaining the purpose and behavior of the rule.

platform.description: Indicates the operating system to which the rule applies. For example, `windows`, `linux`, or `macos`

module_ids.description: A list of module ids associated with the rule.

conditions.description: A combination of parameters configured during rule creation.

path.description: Path to the required files or folders

profile_ids.description: A list of profile ids to which the rule is applied.

status.description: Status of the rule (for example, `enabled` or `disabled`).

scope.description: Specifies the scope of the rule, such as `global` (All endpoints) or `profile` (Exception profiles).

rule_ids.description: The unique identifier of the Disable Prevention rule.
